Title: Days after heatwave, French government cuts climate change adaptation funds
Subhead: The Green Fund, which helps local authorities finance environmental protection and climate adaptation projects, is facing a €162.5 million cut, a nearly 20% reduction.

Thomas Piketty's lab proposes ambitious plan to cut global inequality and curb climate change
The faces of anti-ICE protesters outside Delaney Hall, a symbol of Trump's migrant deportation policy
Subhead: Protests have been ongoing since May 23 outside the Newark migrant detention center in New Jersey, denouncing what activists describe as unsanitary and inhumane conditions for incarcerated migrants.

Title: Zelensky's rehabilitation of controversial Ukrainian nationalists sparks anger in Israel and Poland
Subhead: By attending a reburial ceremony for Andriy Melnyk, a pro-independence leader who collaborated with Nazi Germany against the Soviet Union, Volodymyr Zelensky has backed the country's evolving memorial politics, which have been focused on opposition to Moscow since the February 2022 invasion.

Title: The past, present and future cinema of director Céline Sciamma
Subhead: From June 3 to 16, the MK2 Bibliothèque movie theater in Paris is hosting the first-ever retrospective dedicated to the filmmaker, featuring all her films, a masterclass and a conversation with actor Adèle Haenel.

Title: The health of French presidents, a state secret
Subhead: A new book unveils the last mysteries surrounding the illnesses of French presidents, especially that of Georges Pompidou (1969-1974), drawing on the diary of one of the president's physicians, Jean Bernard.

Bayeux Tapestry's transport to British Museum will be safe, detailed study determines
Subhead: Before the fragile 70-meter-long embroidery is transported to the United Kingdom in July, a new risk assessment study released on Wednesday has given the move the green light.

Title: The 20 French cities where the country's wealthiest live
Subhead: The city of Neuilly-sur-Seine outside Paris has the highest proportion of very wealthy households in France, according to a study by the Inequality Observatory published on Tuesday. The ranking is dominated by the western suburbs of Paris and the areas surrounding Geneva.

Title: Edgar Morin: 'I doubt humanity even as I believe in it'
Subhead: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.
